zh.lifeSummary: Richard Phillpott Alias Milward是John Millard和Agnes Bedells的兒子，於1452年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾沃頓 武安德 艾其。他於1477年與Cateryne Philpott在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾艾其結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1521年6月17日在他的家鄉享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾沃頓 武安德 艾其。

nameMeanings.name: Milward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Millward
meanings: English (mainly West Midlands): occupational name from Middle English milne-ward, mille-ward ‘keeper of a mill, miller’ (Old English mylen-weard). In southern England and the West Midlands this was a standard medieval term for a miller. Compare Miller .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. nameMeanings.name: Phillpott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Alias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Rare, and of uncertain origin, possibly from the surname Amias, which is a local name for someone from Amiens in France. However, both surname and given name are rare. The ending -ias is found in biblical names (e.g. Tobias ), where it represents a Greek form of Hebrew -iyah ‘God’ Amias may sometimes have been chosen in the belief that it was a biblical name, reinforced by the fact that am- is the Latin root meaning ‘love’. See also Amiaz and Amyas .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Phillpott Alias Milward was born in 1452, in Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, England as the son of John Millard and Agnes Bedells. He married Cateryne Philpott in 1477, in Edge, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 17 June 1521, in his hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Wotton under Edge, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Phillpott Alias Milwardは1452年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアウオットオン ウンダー エッジでJohn MillardとAgnes Bedellsの息子として生まれた。1477年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアエッジでCateryne Philpottと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1521年6月17日に故郷で69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアウオットオン ウンダー エッジにて埋葬された。

ko.Wotton Under Edge, Gloucestershire, , En: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 보턴 운더 에지
ko.Wotton-under-Edge, Stroud District, Glou: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 보턴 운더 에지
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Phillpott Alias Milward(은)는 1452년 영국 그루스스터실르 보턴 운더 에지에서 John Millard(와)과 Agnes Bedells의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1477년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 에지에서 Cateryne Philpott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1521년 6월 17일 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 보턴 운더 에지에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Phillpott Alias Milward родился в 1452 г. в местности воттон ундир эдж, глоусестиршир, Англия. Его родителей звали John Millard и Agnes Bedells. Он женился на женщине по имени Cateryne Philpott в 1477 г. в местности эдж, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 17 июня 1521 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности воттон ундир эдж, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
